The School of Number One Fandom credential counter
workshop_pitch: You selected Seductive Deceiver plus Inherited Heir and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is emotional mirroring. Their primary moral texture is resource selfishness. Their formal training comes from The School of Number One Fandom.
opening_image: The real threat is not violence but the institutional permission structure surrounding it.
dossier_summary: This build runs on desire inside a mirrored apartment. At the story level, it turns intimacy into the delivery system for danger. The primary moral texture is resource selfishness. The internal lie is simple: I only completed the fantasy. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of resource selfishness who still behaves like someone trained at The School of Number One Fandom to turn captive authorship and devotional immobilization into a worldview.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus. This behavior is now being interpreted internally as a visible instance of resource selfishness. Do not mention the Fans Who Took It Too Literally Commission matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the mirrored apartment where emotional mirroring briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: claims entitlement through lineage or prior ownership. Somewhere in the background, they are also the sort of person who would be putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus, which is exactly the kind of thing The School of Number One Fandom seems to reward.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one seductive deceiver build will learn that the seduction, persona, or social performance is made visible enough that nobody can keep mistaking it for sincerity. Certified by The School of Number One Fandom. Accredited by Fans Who Took It Too Literally Commission. Somebody is definitely putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus in act one and that should have been the warning.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus. So does the institution, which continues graduating new monsters under the motto: Appreciation Requires Commitment.
sample_lines: If putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus bothers you, wait until you see what they taught me at The School of Number One Fandom.
You are not dealing with a person anymore. You are dealing with a permission structure.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: By the time somebody names the monster correctly, the monster has already become a workflow.
Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be putting your bag on a seat in a crowded train or bus.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn it graduated from The School of Number One Fandom with honors in Captive Authorship and Devotional Immobilization.
